summary: Comprehensive testing of Bariatric Istanbul medical tourism landing page. All 26 backend API tests passed (100%). Frontend fully functional with all Spanish content sections, admin CMS, blog, and lead capture form working correctly.

issues: Database contains old English content from previous session. Seed data only runs on empty collections. Admin can update via CMS.

passed_tests: Backend: API root returns Spanish message
Backend: Admin login with correct credentials (admin@bariatricistanbul.com / admin123)
Backend: Login rejects invalid credentials (401)
Backend: /me endpoint requires auth token
Backend: /me endpoint returns user data with valid token
Backend: GET transformations returns seeded data
Backend: GET testimonials returns seeded data
Backend: GET video-testimonials returns seeded data
Backend: GET itinerary returns 6 days sorted by day_number
Backend: GET surgeon returns single-item with Spanish content
Backend: GET hospital returns single-item with Spanish content
Backend: GET blog returns 3 SEO-optimized posts
Backend: GET blog by slug works correctly
Backend: GET blog with published_only filter works
Backend: Blog posts have all SEO fields (meta_title, meta_description, keywords)
Backend: Sitemap XML endpoint returns valid XML with blog URLs
Backend: All CRUD operations require authentication
Backend: Create and delete transformation with auth works
Backend: Create and delete testimonial with auth works
Backend: Create and delete blog post with auth works
Frontend: Homepage loads with Spanish hero text 'Manga Gástrica en Estambul'
Frontend: Logo image displayed in navigation
Frontend: All 9 navigation links present (Nosotros, Hospital, Cirujano, Procedimientos, Itinerario, Resultados, Testimonios, FAQ, Blog)
Frontend: Lead capture form with name, surname, email, international phone, language selector
Frontend: WhatsApp floating button present
Frontend: All 8 page sections exist (#nosotros, #hospital, #cirujano, #procedimientos, #itinerario, #resultados, #testimonios, #faq)
Frontend: Hospital section displays dynamic content from API
Frontend: Surgeon section displays Dr. Mehmet Yılmaz with credentials
Frontend: Procedures section shows 4 surgery options in Spanish
Frontend: Itinerary section shows 6-day timeline
Frontend: FAQ section with Spanish questions and accordion
Frontend: Footer with logo, Admin link, Blog link
Frontend: Blog listing page shows 3 published posts
Frontend: Blog post page renders HTML content with CTA
Frontend: Admin login page with Spanish labels
Frontend: Admin login redirects to /admin/dashboard
Frontend: Admin dashboard has all 7 tabs (Transformaciones, Testimonios, Videos, Itinerario, Cirujano, Hospital, Blog)
Frontend: Admin Blog tab shows 3 posts with edit/delete buttons
Frontend: Admin Surgeon tab shows single-item with Edit button
Frontend: Admin Hospital tab shows single-item with Edit button
Frontend: Admin Blog form has all SEO fields (Título, Slug, Extracto, Contenido, Meta Título, Meta Descripción, Keywords, Publicado)
Frontend: Admin Surgeon form has all fields (Nombre, Título/Cargo, Biografía, Cirugías, Experiencia, Credenciales)
Frontend: Admin Hospital form has all fields (Nombre, Descripción, Características)
Frontend: Admin logout works correctly
critical_code_review_comments: ESLint warning: useEffect missing dependency 'loadData' in AdminDashboard (Line 686) - minor, can be fixed with eslint-disable comment or useCallback
Database seed data only runs on empty collections - existing English data persists. This is expected behavior for CMS apps.
seed_data_creation: Backend seeds Spanish content for: transformations (4), testimonials (3), video-testimonials (3), itinerary (6 days), surgeon (1), hospital (1), blog (3 SEO posts). Only runs if collections are empty.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All features fully tested and working. Backend API tests in /app/backend/tests/test_bariatric_api.py cover auth, all content types, and sitemap. Admin CMS fully functional with localStorage Bearer token auth. Blog pages use React Router. Database has old English content from previous session - admin can update via CMS.
